title: Secretary Spec — Sub-Agent Fleet
status: drafted — not yet built; 21 specialist agent designs already exist in framework work
spec_type: subsystem + directory layout + registry + delegation mechanism
core_claim: Secretary is orchestrator without a fleet. Sub-agents with jurisdiction contracts, failure modes, and handoff grammar are the execution-quality layer. Orchestrator handles framing and commitment; sub-agents handle artisan execution.

directory_layout.per_agent: agents/<agent_name>/agents.md declaring jurisdiction, inputs, outputs, failure_modes, escalation_rules

delegation_mechanism: Orchestrator hands typed task → sub-agent returns typed result OR typed abstention (framework-lawful jurisdiction-out-of-bounds).
first_v1_agents: Drafting — message drafts typed by channel (sms/email/slack/imessage)
Voice-audit — enforces voice model (blocked: voice model not loaded)

upstream: Voice model not yet loaded (blocks voice-audit agent)
21 specialist agent specs location — need to pull jurisdiction contracts into agents/ (OPEN QUESTION)
typed_abstention_first_class commitment already declared — semantics inherited
downstream: All Secretary subsystems that need artisan execution (drafting, research, voice audit, calendar)

structural_claim: Sub-agent fleet is the test of orchestrator-as-stage-selector generality. If delegation mechanism holds across drafting + red-team + calendar agents with typed results and typed abstentions, primitive generalizes. If it fragments into per-agent special cases, it doesn't.
open_questions: v1 subset of the ~21: which ship immediately vs. stay spec-only?
Agent versioning: shared with agents.md commit vs. independent lifecycle?
Handoff graph encoding: per-agent vs. centralized in registry?
Typed-result schema: per-agent vs. unified wrapper with per-agent payload?
Model tier selection: declared by agent vs. picked by orchestrator at delegation time?
